message:
bd-24f: PackageManager tests + ResolveRoots

Add integration tests for PackageManager parsing/paths/filters; expose deterministic resource de-dupe ordering; extend legacy capture args; fix compaction test expectation.

 {"id":"bd-22h","title":"Design stratified sampling matrix for extension diversity","description":"Background:
- The sample must cover extension feature variety, not just popularity.

Include axes such as:
- Capabilities used: read/write/exec/http/env.
- Interaction model: tool-only vs slash commands vs event hooks.
- Runtime tier: WASM vs JS bundle vs MCP/process.
- Complexity: single-file vs multi-module; config-heavy vs config-light.
- I/O patterns: filesystem-heavy, network-heavy, CPU-heavy.

Output:
- A sampling matrix/table that maps each candidate extension to axes, with quotas per axis.

Acceptance:
- Matrix makes it explicit why each selected extension is in the sample.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:21:15.663936275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:47:39.873324254Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T05:47:39.873260104Z","close_reason":"Stratified sampling matrix documented in docs/EXTENSION_SAMPLING_MATRIX.md","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22h","depends_on_id":"bd-2uv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:33:46.115721353Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-22h","depends_on_id":"bd-gx1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:33:37.446436457Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":15,"issue_id":"bd-22h","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added docs/EXTENSION_SAMPLING_MATRIX.md with axis quotas (target=16) and full candidate tag mapping using docs/EXTENSION_CANDIDATES.md + CONFORMANCE.md criteria.","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:47:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-22p","title":"Workstream: Theme System Implementation","description":"# Workstream: Theme System Implementation

## Goal
Implement theme loading and switching for the TUI, matching Pi Agent's theme system.

## Background
The TypeScript Pi Agent supports themes that control:
- Color palette (foreground, background, accent colors)
- Syntax highlighting colors
- Status line appearance
- Thinking block styling

Themes are stored as JSON files in:
- ~/.pi/agent/themes/ (global)
- .pi/themes/ (project)
- Package themes (installed via pi install)

## Scope

### In Scope
1. Theme file format definition (JSON schema)
2. Theme discovery from standard locations
3. Theme application to TUI components
4. Theme switching via /theme command
5. Default themes (dark, light, solarized)

### Out of Scope
1. Theme creation wizard
2. Real-time theme editing
3. Complex inheritance chains

## Theme Format
```json
{
  "name": "ocean-dark",
  "version": "1.0",
  "colors": {
    "foreground": "#d4d4d4",
    "background": "#1e1e1e",
    "accent": "#007acc",
    "success": "#4ec9b0",
    "warning": "#ce9178",
    "error": "#f44747",
    "muted": "#6a6a6a"
  },
  "syntax": {
    "keyword": "#569cd6",
    "string": "#ce9178",
    "number": "#b5cea8",
    "comment": "#6a9955",
    "function": "#dcdcaa"
  },
  "ui": {
    "border": "#3c3c3c",
    "selection": "#264f78",
    "cursor": "#aeafad"
  }
}
```

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Theme Loading
- Parse theme JSON files
- Discover themes from standard locations
- Validate theme format
- Provide default themes

### Phase 2: Theme Application
- Apply colors to lipgloss styles
- Wire syntax colors to glamour
- Update all TUI components

### Phase 3: Theme Switching
- /theme command to list/switch
- Persist theme preference in settings
- Hot-reload on theme file change (optional)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Theme files parsed correctly
- [ ] Default themes work
- [ ] /theme command works
- [ ] TUI renders with theme colors
- [ ] Settings remembers theme preference

## Files to Create
- src/theme.rs (theme loading/application)
- themes/dark.json (default dark theme)
- themes/light.json (default light theme)

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs (apply theme to TUI)
- src/tui.rs (apply theme to console output)
- src/config.rs (theme preference setting)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:39:16.349318511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:39:16.356035177Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22p","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:39:16.356001444Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-24f","title":"Unit tests: PackageManager path resolution + filters","description":"Goal:
- Add unit tests for PackageManager path resolution, package identity parsing, and resource filters using real temp dirs, with detailed logging.

Scope:
- parse_source for npm/git/local and identity normalization.
- Installed path resolution for user/project scopes.
- Resource filters: extensions/skills/prompts/themes enable/disable behavior.
- Deterministic resolution ordering and error paths.

Logging Requirements:
- Use TestHarness/TestLogger (bd-3ml) in each test.
- Log parsed source components, resolved paths, and filter decisions.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tests use real temp dirs and filesystem paths.
- No mocks; no network access.
- Edge cases covered (pinned refs, invalid sources, missing dirs).
- Logs include inputs + resolved outputs for debugging.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:58:46.313157289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:27:49.086704616Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24f","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:58:46.322467059Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-24f","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:27:49.086677235Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-25q","title":"Extension compatibility proof: popular sample + conformance harness","description":"Goal:
- Prove that a large, diverse, *popular* sample of existing pi-agent extensions runs "as-is" with full behavioral fidelity in pi_agent_rust.
- Produce evidence that is testable, repeatable, and automated (fixtures + harness + reports).

Scope notes:
- "As-is" means no changes to extension source code or packaging, except for standard install/build steps that the legacy pi toolchain already requires.
- Compatibility must be demonstrated across tools, slash commands, and event hooks where provided.
- Evidence must include deterministic conformance fixtures and a runner that compares Rust output to legacy output.

Key references (for future self):
- EXTENSIONS.md (design goals, runtime tiers).
- docs/schema/extension_protocol.json + docs/wit/extension.wit (protocol spec).
- EXISTING_PI_STRUCTURE.md sections 7, 9, 10 for CLI/resource flow.

Exit criteria:
- Workstream summaries (sample, capture, runtime, harness, benchmarks, docs) are each marked done.
- The conformance harness passes for the entire selected sample.
- A written report maps each extension to a pass/fail status and notes any deviations.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:19:34.839197122Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:33:25.486349564Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-25q","depends_on_id":"bd-155","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:33:25.486326902Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-25q","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:32:58.178295362Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-25q","depends_on_id":"bd-20p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:33:15.291926668Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-25q","depends_on_id":"bd-269","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:33:06.528370559Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-25q","depends_on_id":"bd-29c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:32:36.705432Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-25q","depends_on_id":"bd-3oq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:32:47.816503957Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-261","title":"Unit tests: extension protocol parsing + schema validation","description":"Background:
- Protocol correctness is foundational; failures here invalidate all runtime/harness results.

Steps:
- Add unit tests for ExtensionMessage parse/validate (happy path + edge cases).
- Round-trip serialize/deserialize for every message type.
- Validate against docs/schema/extension_protocol.json (schema compliance tests).
- Include negative cases (missing fields, wrong version, invalid types).

Logging requirements:
- Tests should emit clear context on failure (message type, field, expected vs actual).

Acceptance:
- All protocol message variants have unit coverage and schema parity tests pass.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:45:39.810448391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T07:32:32.921367204Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-261","depends_on_id":"bd-576","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:48:03.729397152Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-26s","title":"Workstream: Comprehensive Test Coverage (No Mocks)","description":"# Workstream: Comprehensive Test Coverage (No Mocks)

## Goal
Achieve 95%+ test coverage using real implementations wherever possible, with
detailed logging for E2E integration tests. Eliminate reliance on mocks for
core functionality testing.

## Background
Current test coverage analysis reveals critical gaps:

### Well Tested (Good)
- Built-in Tools: 100% coverage (122 conformance fixture cases)
- Session Tree Logic: 11 tests covering branching/navigation
- SSE Parser: 9 tests covering all edge cases
- Auth Flow: 3 tests for PKCE/OAuth setup

### Critical Gaps (Bad)
- **Provider Streaming**: 0 tests with real API/streaming (only unit tests)
- **Interactive TUI**: 2 tests out of 2842 lines (<0.1%)
- **CLI/main.rs**: 0 tests for 1581 lines
- **Compaction**: 0 tests for 903 lines
- **Configuration**: 0 tests for 332 lines
- **Session Persistence**: Only serialization tested, not full I/O cycle

### What "No Mocks" Means
- Provider tests: Use VCR-style recorded responses, not fake providers
- File tests: Use real temp directories (already done)
- CLI tests: Use actual binary execution
- TUI tests: Use terminal emulator or snapshot testing
- Network tests: Use local test server or recorded responses

## Testing Strategy

### 1. Provider Streaming Tests (VCR Style)
Record real API responses and replay them:
- No external network calls during CI
- Real response parsing (not simplified mocks)
- Error scenarios from real API errors

### 2. E2E Integration Tests
Full workflow tests with verbose logging:
- Start pi binary with arguments
- Verify output and exit codes
- Check session files created
- Log every step for debugging

### 3. Snapshot Testing for TUI
Capture terminal output and compare:
- Render TUI components to string
- Compare against golden snapshots
- Update snapshots on intentional changes

### 4. Property-Based Testing
Use proptest for edge cases:
- Truncation with random inputs
- Session serialization round-trips
- Path resolution edge cases

### 5. Compaction Tests
- Window selection + summary insertion
- Tool/thinking/image content handling
- Session invariants preserved after compaction

### 6. Configuration Tests
- Config discovery + precedence rules
- Env + CLI override behavior
- Invalid config errors with actionable messages

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Provider streaming tested with recorded responses
- [ ] TUI logic tested (state transitions, rendering)
- [ ] CLI argument parsing fully tested
- [ ] Compaction logic tested
- [ ] Configuration loading tested
- [ ] Session persistence cycles tested
- [ ] E2E tests with verbose logging exist
- [ ] CI runs all tests in <5 minutes

## Files to Create
- tests/provider_streaming.rs - VCR-style provider tests
- tests/e2e_cli.rs - CLI integration tests
- tests/tui_snapshot.rs - TUI snapshot tests
- tests/compaction.rs - Compaction pipeline tests
- tests/config_loading.rs - Config discovery/override tests
- tests/fixtures/vcr/*.json - Recorded API responses

## Dependencies
None (can start immediately, independent of HTTP migration)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:34:05.966405126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:46:20.507382886Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26s","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:34:05.973753960Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Archived artifacts (tarballs/zip) stored under a dedicated fixtures area with checksums.

Definition of done:
- Sample list is final and frozen with versions.
- Artifacts are locally archived with checksums.
- License/redistribution policy applied to each artifact (OK / restricted / exclude).

Dependencies:

 {"id":"bd-2i5","title":"Wire extension runtime into agent loop (tools/commands/events)","description":"Background:
- Extensions must integrate with the agent loop so their tools and commands are callable.

Steps:
- Register extension tools with the ToolRegistry and route tool_call/tool_result.
- Register slash commands and hook them into interactive mode.
- Implement event hook emission at appropriate agent lifecycle points.
- Ensure wiring supports deterministic test hooks (ordering + timing).

Logging requirements:
- Emit structured logs for tool registration, command invocation, and event hooks (correlation IDs).

Acceptance:
- An extension-provided tool and slash command can be invoked end-to-end.
- Logs follow the extension logging spec for conformance/E2E tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:24:12.588180426Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:07:05.988120840Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2i5","depends_on_id":"bd-1e0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:36:42.381852755Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2i5","depends_on_id":"bd-320","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:36:33.786661150Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2i5","depends_on_id":"bd-3d1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:36:24.409519415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2i5","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:58:11.702297011Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2jh","title":"Implement error handling path tests","description":"# Implement error handling path tests

## Goal
Test all error conditions that can occur during normal operation, with detailed logging.

## Background
Error paths are currently untested but critical for UX:
- Network failures
- API rate limits
- Authentication failures
- Malformed responses
- File system errors
- Timeout scenarios

## Runtime + Determinism
- Do not introduce new tokio-only tests; use `#[asupersync::test]` or a runtime wrapper.
- All API error cases must use VCR playback (no live network).
- File system errors must use temp dirs and deterministic paths.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log cassette path, error category, and sanitized error details.
- On failure, dump expected vs actual error variants + any response body fragments.

## Test Categories

### Network Errors
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_connection_refused() {
    let vcr = VcrRecorder::new("network_connection_refused");
    let provider = AnthropicProvider::new_with_client(vcr.client());

    let result = provider.stream(&context, &options).await;
    assert!(matches!(result, Err(Error::Network { .. })));
}
```

### API Errors
- 429 rate limit (retry-after)
- 401 auth failure
- 403 forbidden
- 400 bad request
- 500 server error
- 529 overloaded

### Malformed Response Errors
- Invalid JSON body
- Missing required fields
- Unexpected SSE event type
- Truncated stream

### File System Errors
- Read permission denied
- Write permission denied
- Disk full (simulate with tempfs limits if feasible)
- Path too long

### Tool Execution Errors
- bash command not found
- bash timeout
- edit file not found

 {"id":"bd-2lg","title":"Implement compaction module unit tests","description":"# Implement compaction module unit tests

## Goal
Add comprehensive unit tests for src/compaction.rs (903 lines, currently 0 tests).

## Background
Compaction is a critical feature that:
- Summarizes old conversation history to save context tokens
- Identifies optimal cut points in message history
- Tracks file operations (read/write/edit) for summary
- Uses LLM to generate summaries

## Module Analysis (src/compaction.rs)

### Key Types
```rust
struct CompactionContext {
    entries: Vec<SessionEntry>,
    config: CompactionConfig,
    token_estimator: TokenEstimator,
}

struct CutPoint {
    entry_index: usize,
    reason: CutPointReason,
    tokens_before: usize,
    tokens_after: usize,
}

enum CutPointReason {
    TokenLimit,
    UserMessageBoundary,
    ToolResultBoundary,
    NaturalBreak,
}

struct FileSummary {
    path: String,
    operations: Vec<FileOp>,
}
```

### Key Functions
- `estimate_tokens(entry: &SessionEntry) -> usize`
- `find_cut_points(entries: &[SessionEntry], config: &Config) -> Vec<CutPoint>`
- `extract_file_operations(entries: &[SessionEntry]) -> Vec<FileSummary>`
- `prepare_compaction(entries: &[SessionEntry], config: &Config) -> CompactionPlan`
- `generate_summary_prompt(entries: &[SessionEntry], files: &[FileSummary]) -> String`

## Test Categories

### 1. Token Estimation Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_estimate_tokens_simple_text() {
    let entry = user_message("Hello, world!");
    let tokens = estimate_tokens(&entry);
    assert!(tokens > 0 && tokens < 10);
}

#[test]
fn test_estimate_tokens_code_block() {
    let entry = assistant_message("```rust\nfn main() {}\n```");
    let tokens = estimate_tokens(&entry);
    // Code blocks should estimate higher
}

#[test]
fn test_estimate_tokens_tool_result() {
    let entry = tool_result("read", "Large file content...");
    let tokens = estimate_tokens(&entry);
    // Verify reasonable estimate
}
```

### 2. Cut Point Detection Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_find_cut_points_token_limit() {
    let entries = generate_entries(100, 1000);  // 100 entries, ~1000 tokens each
    let config = CompactionConfig { max_tokens: 50000 };
    
    let cuts = find_cut_points(&entries, &config);
    
    // Should find cuts when exceeding limit
    assert!(!cuts.is_empty());
    assert!(cuts[0].tokens_after <= config.max_tokens);
}

#[test]
fn test_cut_point_prefers_user_message_boundary() {
    let entries = vec![
        user_message("Q1"),
        assistant_message("A1"),
        user_message("Q2"),  // Preferred cut point
        assistant_message("A2"),
    ];
    
    let cuts = find_cut_points(&entries, &low_limit_config());
    
    // Should cut at user message boundary
    assert_eq!(cuts[0].reason, CutPointReason::UserMessageBoundary);
}

#[test]
fn test_cut_point_preserves_tool_pairs() {
    let entries = vec![
        user_message("Read file"),
        assistant_with_tool_call("read", { "path": "test.txt" }),
        tool_result("read", "File contents..."),
        assistant_message("The file contains..."),
    ];
    
    let cuts = find_cut_points(&entries, &config);
    
    // Should not cut between tool call and result
    for cut in cuts {
        assert_ne!(cut.entry_index, 2);  // Not after tool call
    }
}
```

### 3. File Operation Extraction Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_extract_file_operations_read() {
    let entries = vec![
        tool_result_with_details("read", json!({"path": "src/main.rs", "lines_read": 100})),
    ];
    
    let files = extract_file_operations(&entries);
    
    assert_eq!(files.len(), 1);
    assert_eq!(files[0].path, "src/main.rs");
    assert_eq!(files[0].operations[0], FileOp::Read { lines: 100 });
}

#[test]
fn test_extract_file_operations_write() {
    let entries = vec![
        tool_result_with_details("write", json!({"path": "new.txt", "bytes_written": 1234})),
    ];
    
    let files = extract_file_operations(&entries);
    
    assert_eq!(files[0].operations[0], FileOp::Write { bytes: 1234 });
}

#[test]
fn test_extract_file_operations_edit() {
    let entries = vec![
        tool_result_with_details("edit", json!({
            "path": "src/lib.rs",
            "old_length": 10,
            "new_length": 15
        })),
    ];
    
    let files = extract_file_operations(&entries);
    
    assert!(matches!(files[0].operations[0], FileOp::Edit { .. }));
}

#[test]
fn test_file_operations_grouped_by_path() {
    let entries = vec![
        tool_result_with_details("read", json!({"path": "a.txt"})),
        tool_result_with_details("edit", json!({"path": "a.txt"})),
        tool_result_with_details("read", json!({"path": "b.txt"})),
    ];
    
    let files = extract_file_operations(&entries);
    
    // Files should be deduplicated
    assert_eq!(files.len(), 2);
    assert_eq!(files.iter().find(|f| f.path == "a.txt").unwrap().operations.len(), 2);
}
```

### 4. Summary Prompt Generation Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_summary_prompt_includes_messages() {
    let entries = vec![
        user_message("Help me with X"),
        assistant_message("I'll help with X by doing Y"),
    ];
    
    let prompt = generate_summary_prompt(&entries, &[]);
    
    assert!(prompt.contains("Help me with X"));
    assert!(prompt.contains("Y"));
}

#[test]
fn test_summary_prompt_includes_file_summary() {
    let files = vec![
        FileSummary {
            path: "src/main.rs".into(),
            operations: vec![FileOp::Read { lines: 100 }, FileOp::Edit { old: 10, new: 15 }],
        },
    ];
    
    let prompt = generate_summary_prompt(&[], &files);
    
    assert!(prompt.contains("src/main.rs"));
    assert!(prompt.contains("read"));
    assert!(prompt.contains("edit"));
}
```

### 5. Compaction Plan Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_prepare_compaction_identifies_entries_to_keep() {
    let entries = generate_entries(50, 1000);
    let config = CompactionConfig {
        max_tokens: 30000,
        keep_recent: 10000,
    };
    
    let plan = prepare_compaction(&entries, &config);
    
    // Should keep recent entries
    assert!(plan.entries_to_keep.len() < entries.len());
    assert!(plan.tokens_after_compaction <= config.max_tokens);
}
```

## Dependencies
None (unit tests only)

## Files
- src/compaction.rs (add #[cfg(test)] module)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 20+ unit tests for compaction
- [ ] Token estimation tested
- [ ] Cut point detection tested
- [ ] File operation extraction tested
- [ ] Summary prompt generation tested
- [ ] Edge cases covered
- [ ] All tests pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:36:53.592325399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:23:36.355281684Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T05:23:36.355215541Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2lg","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:36:53.598326111Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-h04","title":"Extensions: connector dispatcher + capability policy","description":"Background:
- Connector dispatcher is the security boundary for all extension runtimes (WASM/JS).
- It defines the canonical host_call/host_result protocol and enforces capability policy.

Scope:
- Implement dispatcher core, capability checks, error mapping, and structured audit logging per the logging spec.
- Provide hooks for policy modes (strict/prompt/permissive) and test harness instrumentation.

Steps:
- Define connector message types + validation; map to internal tool/command/env/fs/http calls.
- Implement allow/deny decision flow with reason codes; enforce timeouts + cancellation (asupersync).
- Emit structured logs (correlation IDs, capability, decision, elapsed).
- Provide deterministic test hooks/mocks for unit + conformance tests.

Acceptance:
- All hostcalls route through the dispatcher with consistent policy enforcement.
- Logs follow the extension logging schema and redact secrets.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:23:49.329723505Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:14:35.827372808Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T04:14:35.827308398Z","close_reason":"Dispatcher: policy enforcement, timeout handling, redacted logging, test hooks","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h04","depends_on_id":"bd-3so","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:57:23.511568828Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"bd-h04","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented hostcall timeouts + redaction + deterministic log overrides in connector dispatcher. -{"id":"bd-hqq","title":"Unit tests: compaction pipeline + summary invariants","description":"# Unit tests: compaction pipeline + summary invariants

## Goal
Add comprehensive tests for compaction logic to ensure summaries are correct,
message windows are respected, and session invariants hold — without mocks.

## Scope
- Compaction window selection (token budget, message count, system prompt rules).
- Summary insertion semantics (where summary is injected, ordering preserved).
- Handling of tool calls, thinking blocks, and image/content blocks.
- Edge cases: tiny budgets, already-compacted sessions, empty assistant response.
- Session integrity: parent/child IDs, current leaf, and compaction entry metadata.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log input message count, token budget, selected span indices, and summary length.
- On failure, dump the compacted timeline (message IDs + types + brief text).

## Determinism Requirements
- Use fixed fixtures for token counts and message content (no randomness).
- No network usage; temp dirs only if persistence is exercised.

## Files
- tests/compaction.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 15+ compaction tests
- [ ] Window selection respects budget and role ordering
- [ ] Summary entry inserted correctly
- [ ] Tool + thinking blocks preserved or summarized as specified
- [ ] Session tree invariants preserved
- [ ] Logs include compaction selection + summary metadata","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:45:29.310486765Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:45:34.801087177Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hqq","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:45:29.317387163Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-hqq","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:45:34.801055578Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ivj","title":"Migrate Azure OpenAI provider to asupersync HTTP","description":"# Migrate Azure OpenAI provider to asupersync HTTP

## Goal
Replace reqwest usage in src/providers/azure.rs with asupersync HTTP client.

## Background
Azure OpenAI provider:
- Azure-hosted OpenAI API
- Custom endpoint URLs (resource.openai.azure.com)
- API version query parameter
- 654 lines, 5 unit tests

## Azure-Specific Considerations
1. **Endpoint**: Custom Azure resource URLs
2. **API Version**: ?api-version=2024-02-01 query param
3. **Authentication**: api-key header (not Bearer token)
4. **Compatible**: Same response format as OpenAI

## Dependencies
- bd-9sa, bd-pwz
- bd-1vo (OpenAI migration - nearly identical)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No reqwest imports
- [ ] 5 tests pass
- [ ] Streaming works with Azure endpoints","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:17.223664792Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:41.925483754Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ivj","depends_on_id":"bd-1vo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:41.925461983Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ivj","depends_on_id":"bd-gi3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:17.233362232Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
